🔬 Science

Earth and Space

Explore our planet Earth and the vast universe beyond!

📚 Unit 8 ⏱️ 10 min read

Unit 8: Earth and Space

Earth is our home, but it is just one small planet in a vast universe. Let us explore our planet and the space beyond!

The Universe

The universe is everything that exists – all matter, energy, planets, stars, galaxies, and empty space.

What is in the Universe?

ObjectDescription
GalaxiesHuge collections of stars, gas, and dust
StarsGiant balls of hot, glowing gas (like our Sun)
PlanetsLarge objects that orbit stars
MoonsObjects that orbit planets
AsteroidsSmall rocky objects (mostly between Mars and Jupiter)
CometsIcy objects with tails when near the sun

💡 Did you know? There are over 100 billion galaxies in the universe!

The Solar System

Our solar system includes the Sun and everything that orbits it.

Components of the Solar System

ComponentNumberDescription
Sun1The star at the center
Planets8Large objects orbiting the Sun
Dwarf planets5 (Pluto, etc.)Smaller than planets
Moons200+Orbit planets
AsteroidsMillionsRocky objects in the asteroid belt
CometsThousandsIcy objects with tails

The Sun

The Sun is a star – a giant ball of hot, glowing gas.

FactInformation
TypeYellow dwarf star
AgeAbout 4.6 billion years
Temperature5,500°C (surface), 15 million°C (core)
Size109 times wider than Earth
Mass333,000 times Earth’s mass
ImportanceProvides light, heat, and energy for life on Earth

⚠️ Never look directly at the Sun! It can permanently damage your eyes.

The Eight Planets

Planets are in this order from the Sun:

PositionPlanetTypeSize (vs Earth)Interesting Fact
1MercuryRockySmallestClosest to Sun, very hot and cold
2VenusRockySimilar to EarthHottest planet, thick toxic clouds
3EarthRocky1×Our home! Only planet with life
4MarsRockyHalf of EarthThe “Red Planet”
5JupiterGas giant11×Largest planet, Great Red Spot
6SaturnGas giant9×Famous for its rings
7UranusIce giant4×Rotates on its side
8NeptuneIce giant4×Strongest winds in solar system

Acronym to Remember Planet Order:

My Very Educated Mother Just Served Us Noodles (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune)

Inner Planets vs. Outer Planets

FeatureInner PlanetsOuter Planets
PositionClosest to SunFarthest from Sun
CompositionRocky (solid surface)Gas/Ice giants (no solid surface)
SizeSmallerLarger
MoonsFewMany
ExamplesMercury, Venus, Earth, MarsJupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune

Earth: Our Home Planet

Earth by the Numbers

FactInformation
Diameter12,742 km
Distance from Sun150 million km
Time to orbit Sun (1 year)365.25 days
Time to spin once (1 day)24 hours
Number of moons1 (The Moon)

Why is Earth Special?

ReasonExplanation
Liquid waterOnly planet with stable liquid water on surface
AtmosphereContains oxygen for breathing
TemperaturePerfect for life (not too hot, not too cold)
LifeOnly known planet with living organisms

Layers of the Earth

LayerDepthCompositionTemperature
Crust0-35 kmSolid rockCool
Mantle35-2,900 kmSemi-solid rockVery hot
Outer Core2,900-5,150 kmLiquid iron and nickelExtremely hot
Inner Core5,150-6,371 kmSolid iron and nickelHottest
Crust (0–35 km)
Mantle (35–2,900 km)
Outer Core (2,900–5,150 km)
Inner Core (5,150–6,371 km)

The Moon

The Moon is Earth’s only natural satellite.

FactInformation
Diameter3,474 km (about 1/4 of Earth)
Distance from Earth384,400 km
Time to orbit Earth27.3 days
Gravity1/6 of Earth’s gravity

Phases of the Moon

The Moon goes through different shapes (phases) as it orbits Earth.

PhaseAppearance
New MoonInvisible (dark side facing us)
Waxing CrescentSmall sliver growing
First QuarterHalf lit
Waxing GibbousMore than half, growing
Full MoonFully lit (bright circle)
Waning GibbousMore than half, shrinking
Last QuarterHalf lit
Waning CrescentSmall sliver shrinking

💡 Waxing = growing. Waning = shrinking.

Day and Night

PhenomenonCauseDuration
DayPart of Earth facing the Sun12 hours (approx.)
NightPart of Earth facing away from Sun12 hours (approx.)

Earth rotates (spins) on its axis once every 24 hours.

Seasons

PhenomenonCauseDuration
SeasonsEarth’s axis is tilted (23.5°) as it orbits the Sun3 months each

Seasons in the Northern Hemisphere

MonthSeasonWhy?
March-MaySpringNorthern Hemisphere tilts toward Sun
June-AugustSummerNorthern Hemisphere tilts most toward Sun
September-NovemberAutumnNorthern Hemisphere tilts away from Sun
December-FebruaryWinterNorthern Hemisphere tilts most away from Sun

💡 Note: When it is summer in the Northern Hemisphere, it is winter in the Southern Hemisphere!

Stars and Constellations

Stars

A star is a giant ball of hot, glowing gas that produces its own light.

FactInformation
Nearest starThe Sun
Next nearest starProxima Centauri (4.2 light years away)
Star colorTells temperature (blue = hottest, red = coolest)

Constellations

A constellation is a group of stars that forms a pattern.

ConstellationName meansBest seenStar pattern
Ursa MajorBig BearAll yearBig Dipper (7 bright stars)
OrionHunterWinter3 stars in a row (belt)
CassiopeiaQueenAll yearW shape
ScorpiusScorpionSummerJ shape

Day and Night Sky

Day SkyNight Sky
Sun (visible)Moon (sometimes)
Blue color (scattered sunlight)Stars
CloudsPlanets
Occasional MoonConstellations
Meteors (shooting stars)

Space Exploration

Important Milestones

YearEventCountry/Organization
1957First satellite (Sputnik 1)USSR
1961First human in space (Yuri Gagarin)USSR
1969First humans on Moon (Neil Armstrong)USA
1990Hubble Space Telescope launchedUSA/ESA
1998International Space Station (ISS) beganMultiple countries
2012First spacecraft in interstellar spaceUSA

💡 Famous quote: “That’s one small step for a man, one giant leap for mankind.” – Neil Armstrong, 1969

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. The Sun is a ____________.
  2. The Earth takes ____________ hours to spin once on its axis.
  3. The Earth takes ____________ days to orbit the Sun once.
  4. Earth’s only natural satellite is the ____________.
  5. Seasons are caused by Earth’s ____________ axis.

Exercise 2: Name the Planet

Identify the planet from the description.

  1. The largest planet → ____________
  2. The planet with famous rings → ____________
  3. The closest planet to the Sun → ____________
  4. The “Red Planet” → ____________
  5. Our home planet → ____________

Exercise 3: True or False

  1. The Sun is the largest star in the universe. (T/F)
  2. The Moon produces its own light. (T/F)
  3. Earth has three moons. (T/F)
  4. Jupiter is a gas giant. (T/F)
  5. A constellation is a group of stars. (T/F)

Exercise 4: Match the Phase

PhaseDescription
New Moona) Fully lit circle
Full Moonb) Invisible
First Quarterc) Half lit (growing)
Crescentd) Small sliver

Exercise 5: Answer in One Sentence

  1. Why do we have day and night?
  2. What causes the seasons?
  3. Why is Earth special in our solar system?

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. star, 2. 24, 3. 365.25, 4. Moon, 5. tilted

Exercise 2: 1. Jupiter, 2. Saturn, 3. Mercury, 4. Mars, 5. Earth

Exercise 3: 1. F, 2. F, 3. F, 4. T, 5. T

Exercise 4: New Moon→b, Full Moon→a, First Quarter→c, Crescent→d

Great work! You have explored Earth and the vast universe beyond. Keep looking up at the stars!

🎯
